name: Nagasaki Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the East China Sea and the surrounding bays of Nagasaki, evoking the prefecture's maritime character
Nagasaki's prefectural emblem and the sky

description: A light blue field charged at the centre with the white prefectural emblem of Nagasaki, a stylised graphic based on the kanji 長 of 'Nagasaki' and the form of a bird in flight evoking peace.
history: Nagasaki's prefectural symbol was adopted on 6 May 1991, replacing an earlier design. The flag uses the symbol in white on a sea-blue field. Nagasaki is closely associated with the international message of peace following the atomic bombing of 9 August 1945.

features.symbolism: the seas and ocean horizons surrounding Nagasaki, a coastal and island prefecture
a stylised emblem of the kanji 長 (naga, 'long') of 長崎 (Nagasaki) within a peace dove or crane motif, evoking peace and the prefecture's name
